walkthrough.title: Unfold the Dystopia
walkthrough.text: Welcome to Euphoria, citizen. Let's build our better dystopia. Start by placing the game board in the center of the table. You will notice four distinct territories on the board: the Euphorian city of gleaming towers, the Subterran underground network, the Wastelander desert outpost, and the Icarite cloud realm floating above it all. There is also a central area with shared spaces. Make sure everyone can reach the board comfortably.

walkthrough.title: Claim Your Color
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a color and takes all components of that color: four worker dice, ten authority stars, and morale and knowledge track markers. The authority stars are what you need to place to win, and your worker dice are literally your workers. Yes, in this dystopia, your citizens are dice. Place two of your worker dice in front of you and set the other two aside for now. Those extra workers can be recruited later in the game.

walkthrough.title: Roll Your Starting Workers
walkthrough.text: Take your two starting worker dice and roll them. These face values represent how much your workers know about the world. A low number means they are blissfully ignorant and compliant. A high number means they are dangerously aware. Keep these dice in front of you showing the values you rolled. This is your starting workforce, citizen. Hopefully they are not too clever.

walkthrough.title: Set Your Tracks
walkthrough.text: Place your morale marker on the starting position of the morale track and your knowledge marker on the starting position of the knowledge track. Morale determines how many cards you can hold in hand. Knowledge determines your threshold for the worker escape check. If your workers get too smart and their combined dice values exceed your knowledge threshold, the smartest one escapes. We will explain that more during rules.

walkthrough.title: Deal Recruit Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the recruit deck. Deal each player one Ethical Recruit card, placed face-up in front of you. This is your active recruit with a publicly known ability. Then deal each player one Hidden Recruit card, placed face-down. This is your secret agent. You can reveal your hidden recruit later in the game when its trigger condition is met to unleash a powerful ability. Each recruit is aligned with one of the four factions.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Starting Resources
walkthrough.text: Based on player count and your recruits' faction allegiances, distribute starting resources to each player. Resources in Euphoria come in two tiers: basic resources are Water, Food, Energy, and Bliss. Commodities are Gold, Stone, and Clay. Place the remaining resource tokens in supply piles near the board where everyone can reach them.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Markets and Artifacts
walkthrough.text: Place the market tiles face-down on the construction zone spaces on the board. These markets will be built during the game, and trust us, you want to help build them. Penalties for non-contributors can be brutal. Then shuffle the artifact card deck and place it face-down in its designated area. Artifact cards come in six delightfully dystopian types: Bear, Bat, Balloons, Bifocals, Book, and Box.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Tunnel Tracks and Miners
walkthrough.text: Place the miner meeples on the starting positions of each faction's tunnel track. These tracks represent the factions digging through the earth, and as they progress, bonuses unlock for everyone aligned with that faction. Finally, place the commodity resources — Gold, Stone, and Clay — in their respective territory markets on the board.

walkthrough.title: Choose First Overseer
walkthrough.text: Pick a starting player by any method your dystopian council prefers. Play proceeds clockwise. You are now ready to exploit your workers and build a better dystopia. Remember: in Euphoria, ignorance is not just bliss, it is a competitive advantage.

summary.title: Choose First Overseer

walkthrough.title: The Overseer's Choice
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you must do exactly one of two things: place a worker or retrieve your workers. That is the entire decision tree at its highest level. Place a worker to take an action on the board, or pull everyone back to regroup. Simple on the surface, deeply strategic underneath. Let's break down both options.

walkthrough.title: Placing a Worker
walkthrough.text: To place a worker, take one of your available worker dice from your personal supply, roll it, and place it on an action space on the board. Then you immediately perform the action of that space. The value you roll matters — some spaces give bonuses for high values, and matching another worker's value on the same space can trigger a doubles bonus. Once placed, that worker stays on the board until you retrieve it.

walkthrough.title: Gathering Basic Resources
walkthrough.text: Three of the four faction territories have generator spaces where you can collect basic resources. Place a worker in the Euphorian territory to gain Energy. Place a worker in the Subterran territory to gain Food. Place a worker in the Wastelander territory to gain Water. The amount you receive may depend on how many workers are already on that space or your current morale level.

walkthrough.title: Acquiring Commodities
walkthrough.text: Each territory also has commodity spaces where you can trade your basic resources for more valuable commodities. The Euphorians deal in Gold, the Subterrans trade Stone, and the Wastelanders work with Clay. These commodities are what you need to construct markets and place your precious authority stars. Think of basic resources as raw materials and commodities as refined goods.

walkthrough.title: The Icarite Cloud Territory
walkthrough.text: The Icarites float above it all in their cloud territory. Here you can gain Bliss — the fourth basic resource — and use it to acquire Artifact cards or perform special Icarite actions. The Icarite territory works differently from the ground-level factions and offers unique paths to placing authority stars. Do not overlook the clouds, citizen.

walkthrough.title: Constructing Markets
walkthrough.text: This is one of the most important mechanics in Euphoria. Construction sites on the board have face-down market tiles. You can place a worker and pay resources to contribute to building a market. When enough resources have been contributed and the market is completed, every player who contributed places an authority star on it. But here is the twist: every player who did NOT contribute suffers the market's penalty for the rest of the game. Penalties can be devastating — things like losing access to certain spaces or paying double for commodities.

walkthrough.title: Advancing Tunnel Tracks
walkthrough.text: Each faction has a tunnel track that progresses as players place workers on tunnel spaces. When a tunnel is completed, all players who contributed gain bonuses and the faction's allegiance milestone triggers. Advancing a faction's tunnel benefits everyone aligned with that faction, so there is an element of cooperative strategy here. But be careful — you might be helping your opponents too.

walkthrough.title: Trading Artifact Sets
walkthrough.text: Artifact cards come in six types: Bear, Bat, Balloons, Bifocals, Book, and Box. By collecting specific combinations of these cards, you can trade them in at the Artifact Market to place an authority star. Different locations on the board require different sets. This is a reliable path to victory that does not depend on what other players are building.

walkthrough.title: Retrieving Workers
walkthrough.text: When you run out of placed workers — or simply want them back — you can spend your turn retrieving. Take ALL of your workers off the board back to your personal supply. Then roll all of them. Now comes the knowledge check: add up the face values of ALL your workers. If the total exceeds your knowledge threshold on the knowledge track, your smartest worker — the one showing the highest value — realizes the dystopia is a sham and escapes. You lose that worker back to the unused pool. Ouch.

walkthrough.title: The Knowledge Check
walkthrough.text: The knowledge check is the core tension of Euphoria. You want more workers because more workers means more actions per cycle. But more workers means higher dice totals, which means a greater risk of losing one. Your knowledge track position determines your threshold — a higher threshold is safer but harder to achieve. You can use certain recruit abilities or pay resources to mitigate knowledge gain. Managing this balance between workforce size and collective awareness is the heart of the game.

walkthrough.title: Morale and Hand Size
walkthrough.text: Your morale track determines how many recruit and artifact cards you can hold in your hand. Higher morale means a larger hand, giving you more options. If your morale drops to zero, you lose cards and suffer penalties. Morale can shift based on game events, recruit abilities, and certain board actions. Keep your citizens just happy enough to be productive, but not so aware that they revolt.

walkthrough.title: Faction Allegiance
walkthrough.text: Each of the four factions — Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander, and Icarite — has an allegiance track. As allegiance advances, milestones are reached that benefit all players whose recruits are aligned with that faction. Allegiance bonuses can include extra resources, authority star placement, and special abilities. Your recruits' faction alignment determines which allegiance tracks benefit you most.

walkthrough.title: The Doubles Bonus
walkthrough.text: When you place a worker on a space where another worker — yours or an opponent's — is already showing the same die value, you get a doubles bonus. This grants you an extra action or resource. It can be extremely valuable, and savvy overseers sometimes choose spaces specifically to fish for doubles. You cannot control what you roll, but you can choose where to place strategically.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: The game ends immediately when any player places their tenth and final authority star. There is no final round, no tiebreaker, no second place. The first player to place all ten stars wins. Stars are placed by constructing markets, trading artifact sets, reaching allegiance milestones, using recruit abilities, and other special board actions. Every decision should funnel toward one question: how do I place my next star?

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Dystopia
walkthrough.text: Let's walk through a practice game with four players: Red, Blue, Green, and Yellow. Everyone has completed setup. Each player has two starting workers rolled and ready, their morale and knowledge markers set, one ethical recruit face-up, one hidden recruit face-down, and starting resources distributed. The market tiles are face-down on the construction zones, and the artifact deck is shuffled. Red goes first.

walkthrough.title: Red Gathers Energy
walkthrough.text: Red takes one of their worker dice, rolls it (getting a 3), and places it on the Euphorian generator space. Red gains Energy from the supply. Energy is the basic resource of the Euphorian territory, and Red will need it to trade for Gold or contribute to market construction later. A solid opening move — getting resources flowing early is key.

walkthrough.title: Blue Heads Underground
walkthrough.text: Blue rolls their worker die (getting a 2) and places it on the Subterran generator space to gain Food. Blue's ethical recruit has a Subterran allegiance, so working in this territory aligns with their faction strategy. Notice the low roll — a 2 is great for keeping knowledge totals low during the next retrieval.

walkthrough.title: Green Seeks Bliss
walkthrough.text: Green decides to visit the Icarite cloud territory. They roll a 5 and place their worker on a cloud space to gain Bliss. The Icarite territory is often overlooked by new players, but Bliss can be traded for artifact cards, which is one of the most flexible paths to placing authority stars.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Starts Building
walkthrough.text: Yellow has a bold strategy. They roll their worker (getting a 4) and place it on a construction site, paying some starting resources to contribute to building a market. The market tile is still face-down, so nobody knows exactly what penalty non-contributors will face. But Yellow is betting that being an early contributor is worth the resource investment.

walkthrough.title: Red's Second Worker
walkthrough.text: Red places their second and final available worker. They roll a 4 and place it on the Wastelander territory to gain Water. Red now has no workers left in their supply, so next turn they will need to retrieve. Having workers spread across different territories gives Red flexibility but also means a potentially risky knowledge check when retrieving.

walkthrough.title: Blue Scores a Doubles Bonus
walkthrough.text: Blue rolls their second worker and gets a 3. They notice that Red's worker on the Euphorian generator also shows a 3. Blue places their worker on that same space and — doubles bonus! Because Blue's die matches Red's die on the same space, Blue gains the normal Energy reward PLUS a bonus action. Doubles bonuses are one of the most satisfying moments in Euphoria.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE: Green Forgets the Knowledge Check
walkthrough.text: Green places their second worker and later retrieves both workers, rolling them. They get a 5 and a 6 — that is a total of 11. Green excitedly starts planning their next placement, but wait! They forgot to check their knowledge threshold. Their knowledge track shows a threshold of 10. Since 11 exceeds 10, Green's highest die (the 6) escapes! That worker goes back to the unused pool. Green is down to one worker. Always do your knowledge check after retrieving, citizen. Ignorance management is survival.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Continues Construction
walkthrough.text: Yellow retrieves their worker and rolls a low 2 — safe and compliant. They place it right back on the construction site with more resources. Yellow is committed to finishing this market. When it completes, Yellow will place an authority star, and everyone else will suffer the penalty. This aggressive construction strategy puts pressure on all other players to pitch in or suffer consequences.

walkthrough.title: Red Trades for Gold
walkthrough.text: Red retrieves both workers, rolls them (3 and 2 — total of 5, well under the threshold, safe), and on the next turn places one on the Euphorian commodity space. Red pays Energy to acquire Gold. Gold is a valuable commodity needed for certain market constructions and some star-placement actions. Red is building up a commodity engine.

walkthrough.title: Blue Reveals Their Hidden Recruit
walkthrough.text: Blue has been waiting for the right moment, and it arrives. Their hidden recruit's trigger condition is met — perhaps they just advanced a specific faction's allegiance or gathered a certain resource. Blue flips their hidden recruit face-up and gains a powerful ongoing ability. Timing recruit reveals is critical. Too early and you waste the element of surprise. Too late and you miss out on turns of benefit.

walkthrough.title: The First Market Completes
walkthrough.text: Yellow places the final resources needed to complete the market. The tile is flipped face-up, revealing its penalty. Yellow places an authority star on the completed market — their first star placed! But Red, Blue, and Green did not contribute. They now suffer the market's penalty for the rest of the game. Maybe it prohibits use of a certain space, or forces them to pay extra resources. This is why you should always watch what is being built.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE: Red Hoards Resources
walkthrough.text: Red has been collecting Gold and Energy for several turns but has not placed any authority stars. Meanwhile, Yellow has placed two stars and Blue has placed one via an artifact set trade. Red is sitting on a pile of commodities but has not converted them into star placements. In Euphoria, resources are means to an end. Hoarding without a clear plan to place stars means you are falling behind while others race toward ten.

walkthrough.title: Green Recovers a Worker
walkthrough.text: Green, still stinging from losing a worker to the knowledge check, takes an action that allows them to recruit their third worker die from the unused pool. Green rolls it into their available supply. Having three workers gives Green more actions per retrieval cycle, but also increases knowledge check risk. Green will need to be more careful about rolling high values from now on.

walkthrough.title: Blue Trades Artifact Sets
walkthrough.text: Blue has been collecting artifact cards from the Icarite territory and other sources. They now have a valid set — say, a Bear, a Bat, and a Book. Blue visits the Artifact Market and trades in the set to place an authority star. This is Blue's second star. Artifact collection is a steady and reliable star-placement strategy that does not depend on other players' construction timing.

walkthrough.title: Faction Allegiance Milestone
walkthrough.text: Through various tunnel advances and board actions, the Subterran allegiance track reaches a milestone. All players with Subterran-aligned recruits benefit. Blue, whose ethical recruit is Subterran, places an authority star as an allegiance bonus. Green's hidden recruit (now revealed) is also Subterran and gains extra resources. Allegiance milestones reward players who have invested in a faction's progress.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Builds a Second Market
walkthrough.text: Yellow continues their aggressive construction strategy and completes a second market. Another authority star placed, and another penalty levied against non-contributors. Yellow now has four stars placed and is clearly in the lead. The other players start feeling the pressure — multiple market penalties are stacking up and constraining their options.

walkthrough.title: MISTAKE: Green Ignores Market Construction
walkthrough.text: Green has been focusing entirely on artifact collection and tunnel advancement, completely ignoring market construction. They now suffer penalties from two completed markets — one prevents them from using a key action space, and another forces them to pay double for a commodity. Green realizes too late that participating in at least some market construction is essential. The penalties compound and make everything else harder. Do not let markets complete without contributing, even if construction is not your main strategy.

walkthrough.title: The Race Intensifies
walkthrough.text: Yellow has six stars placed. Blue has four. Red has finally started converting resources to stars and has three. Green, hampered by market penalties and a lost worker, has two. The game is reaching its climax. Every turn feels critical now. Players are calculating the fastest path to their remaining stars — markets, artifacts, allegiance milestones, recruit abilities. One big turn could change everything.

walkthrough.title: Blue Makes a Push
walkthrough.text: Blue chains together an artifact set trade for a star, then on the following turn uses a recruit ability to place another star. Blue jumps from four to six stars in two turns. The race is now neck-and-neck with Yellow. Both are at six stars with four to go. Red and Green start playing kingmaker, deciding whose allegiance tracks to advance and whose construction sites to contribute to.

walkthrough.title: Yellow's Final Sprint
walkthrough.text: Yellow completes a third market (star seven), trades an artifact set (star eight), and reaches an allegiance milestone (star nine). Yellow needs just one more star. The tension is palpable. On Yellow's next turn, they place a worker on a special board space that allows star placement by paying commodities. Yellow pays their last Gold and places their tenth authority star. The game ends immediately. Yellow wins!

walkthrough.title: What We Learned
walkthrough.text: Let's recap the key lessons from this practice game. First, the knowledge check is real — Green lost a worker by ignoring it, and that setback cascaded through the rest of the game. Second, hoarding resources without converting them to stars is a losing strategy, as Red discovered. Third, market construction is not optional — Green's multiple penalties crippled their efficiency. And fourth, diversifying your star-placement methods gives you flexibility, as Blue demonstrated by using artifacts, allegiance, and recruit abilities.

walkthrough.title: Before You Build Your Dystopia
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your own game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to factions, resources, and game terms.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Happy oppressing, citizen!

walkthrough.title: Keep Your Workers Ignorant
walkthrough.text: The single most important concept in Euphoria is managing your workers' knowledge. When you retrieve workers, you roll all of them and sum their values. If the total exceeds your knowledge threshold, you lose your highest die. Losing a worker is devastating because it means fewer actions per retrieval cycle. Prefer low rolls, and be cautious about recruiting a third or fourth worker unless your knowledge threshold can handle the increased totals.

walkthrough.title: Never Ignore Market Construction
walkthrough.text: Markets are the game's most powerful offensive and defensive mechanism. Contributing to a market earns you an authority star. Failing to contribute saddles you with a permanent penalty. Even if construction is not your primary strategy, always keep an eye on what is being built. Contributing a few resources to avoid a penalty is almost always worth it. Think of it as paying protection money in a dystopia — because that is exactly what it is.

walkthrough.title: Diversify Your Star Placement
walkthrough.text: Do not rely on a single method to place all ten stars. Markets, artifact sets, faction allegiance, recruit abilities, and special board actions all offer star placement opportunities. If you over-commit to one path and it gets blocked or slows down, you need alternatives. The most successful overseers spread their efforts across at least two or three star-placement methods.

walkthrough.title: Time Your Hidden Recruit Reveal
walkthrough.text: Your hidden recruit is one of your most powerful assets. Revealing it too early wastes turns of surprise and might alert opponents to your strategy. Revealing it too late means you miss out on turns of benefit. Study the trigger condition and plan your game around it. The ideal reveal creates an immediate advantage that is hard for opponents to counter.

walkthrough.title: Hunt for Doubles Bonuses
walkthrough.text: When you roll a worker and its value matches another worker already on your target space, you get a doubles bonus — an extra action or resource. This can be incredibly valuable, essentially giving you two actions for the price of one. While you cannot control your roll, you can choose spaces where a match is possible. Always scan the board for potential doubles before deciding where to place.

walkthrough.title: Convert Resources Efficiently
walkthrough.text: Resources in Euphoria exist in a clear hierarchy: basic resources convert to commodities, and commodities convert to authority stars. Do not sit on a stockpile of basic resources when you could be upgrading them. And do not hoard commodities when you could be placing stars. Every resource you hold is a star you have not placed yet. In a race game, tempo is everything.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents' Stars
walkthrough.text: Euphoria ends immediately when someone places their tenth star. Keep a constant count of every player's placed stars. If someone is at seven or eight, you need to either race faster or find ways to slow them down — perhaps by competing for the same construction sites or advancing allegiance tracks that help you more than them. Situational awareness wins dystopias.

walkthrough.title: Leverage Faction Alignment
walkthrough.text: Your recruits' faction alignments are not just flavor text. They determine which allegiance milestones benefit you. If both your ethical and hidden recruits share a faction, you benefit doubly from that faction's progress. If they are different factions, you have broader coverage. Pay attention to which factions other players are advancing and hitch a ride on their progress when your alignment matches.

summary.title: Keep Your Workers Ignorant
bullets: Sum of all worker dice must stay under knowledge threshold
Losing a worker = fewer actions per cycle = major setback
Be cautious about recruiting 3rd/4th workers

summary.title: Never Ignore Market Construction
bullets: Contributing = authority star; not contributing = permanent penalty
Even partial contribution can avoid penalties
Think of it as paying dystopian protection money

summary.title: Diversify Your Star Placement
bullets: Use 2-3 methods: markets, artifacts, allegiance, recruits, board actions
Over-committing to one path is risky if blocked
Flexibility wins the race

summary.title: Time Your Hidden Recruit Reveal
bullets: Too early = wasted surprise; too late = missed benefit
Study the trigger condition and plan around it
Ideal reveal creates an immediate, hard-to-counter advantage

summary.title: Hunt for Doubles Bonuses
bullets: Matching die value on same space = bonus action/resource
Cannot control rolls, but can choose spaces strategically
Scan the board for potential matches before placing

summary.title: Convert Resources Efficiently
bullets: Basic resources -> commodities -> authority stars
Do not hoard — every held resource is an unplaced star
Tempo is everything in a race game

summary.title: Watch Your Opponents' Stars
bullets: Game ends immediately at 10 stars — no final round
Track every player's star count constantly
Race faster or find ways to slow the leader

summary.title: Leverage Faction Alignment
bullets: Recruits determine which allegiance milestones benefit you
Same-faction recruits = double benefit from one track
Different factions = broader coverage across tracks

walkthrough.title: The Market Bully
walkthrough.text: One of the strongest strategies in Euphoria is racing to complete markets that punish your opponents. Identify markets with the most crippling penalties and pour your resources into completing them quickly. If you can finish two or three markets before opponents contribute, you gain stars while they gain debilitating restrictions. This is especially powerful in the early game when other players are focused on building resource engines. Be the builder of the dystopia, not its victim.

walkthrough.title: Knowledge Threshold Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Advanced players manage their knowledge threshold aggressively. By increasing your threshold through game actions, you can safely field three or four workers while opponents are stuck with two. More workers means more actions per cycle, which translates to faster star placement. Invest in threshold increases early — the compound benefit of extra actions over many turns far outweighs the initial cost. Just be aware that threshold management requires consistent attention.

walkthrough.title: Faction Tunnel Timing
walkthrough.text: Tunnel completion triggers allegiance milestones that benefit all aligned players. Advanced timing means contributing to tunnels when you are positioned to benefit most. If you have two recruits aligned with the same faction, you benefit doubly from that faction's tunnel completion. Conversely, avoid advancing tunnels that primarily benefit your opponents. Sometimes the best move is letting others do the digging while you reap the rewards through alignment alone.

walkthrough.title: The Artifact Collector
walkthrough.text: Artifact sets provide a reliable, opponent-independent path to stars. Advanced artifact collectors use the Icarite cloud territory to generate Bliss, trade Bliss for artifact cards, and quickly assemble sets. The key insight is that artifact collection scales well — the more cards you see, the more sets you can complete. High morale helps here because it increases your hand size, letting you hold more cards while assembling the right combinations.

walkthrough.title: Recruit Synergy
walkthrough.text: Your two recruits are not independent cards — they form a synergy package. Advanced players consider how their ethical and hidden recruits interact. Do they share a faction, doubling your allegiance benefits? Does one generate resources that the other's ability needs? Does the hidden recruit's trigger condition align with your overall strategy? The best games often hinge on unlock moments where both recruits' abilities combine for explosive star placement.

walkthrough.title: Worker Placement as Denial
walkthrough.text: In Euphoria, placing a worker on a space can deny that space to opponents — or at least change the economics. Advanced players consider not just what they gain from a placement, but what opponents lose. Blocking a key commodity space right before an opponent needs it can be as valuable as gaining the commodity yourself. This is especially effective in two- or three-player games where space competition is tighter.

walkthrough.title: Retrieval Timing
walkthrough.text: Most players retrieve when they run out of workers. Advanced players sometimes retrieve early — before all workers are placed — to reset their positions and re-evaluate. Early retrieval can surprise opponents who expected you to take another action. It also gives you first access to contested spaces in the next cycle. The risk is fewer actions in the current cycle, but the positional advantage can be worth it.

walkthrough.title: Morale Investment
walkthrough.text: Morale determines your hand size, and hand size determines your strategic flexibility. Advanced players who plan artifact-heavy or recruit-dependent strategies invest in morale early. A larger hand lets you hold more artifact cards, increasing the chances of completing sets quickly. It also lets you hold recruits from special actions without being forced to discard. Do not let morale drop to zero unless you have deliberately built a low-morale strategy.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Table
walkthrough.text: In a multiplayer race, reading the table is as important as executing your own strategy. Watch which factions other players are aligned with and predict their allegiance moves. Track who is contributing to which markets and anticipate completions. Notice who is collecting artifacts and estimate their hand composition. The player who sees the game end coming two turns before it arrives can position themselves to either win or at least maximize their final star placements.

walkthrough.title: The Blitz Endgame
walkthrough.text: The most dangerous players in Euphoria are the ones who chain multiple star placements in a single cycle. A blitz endgame might look like: place a worker to complete a market (star), retrieve, place a worker to trade an artifact set (star), then on the next cycle use a recruit ability (star) and reach an allegiance milestone (star). Going from six stars to ten in two cycles gives opponents almost no time to react. Build toward this kind of explosive finish by stockpiling the right resources and cards, then strike all at once. In a dystopia, the best revolutions happen suddenly.

summary.title: The Market Bully
bullets: Race to complete markets with the harshest penalties
Gain stars while opponents gain restrictions
Most effective in the early game before others can react

summary.title: Knowledge Threshold Manipulation
bullets: Invest early in increasing your knowledge threshold
Safely field 3-4 workers = more actions per cycle
Compound benefit over many turns outweighs initial cost

summary.title: Faction Tunnel Timing
bullets: Contribute to tunnels when positioned to benefit most
Double-aligned recruits double your milestone benefits
Sometimes let others dig while you reap alignment rewards

summary.title: The Artifact Collector
bullets: Use Icarite territory for Bliss -> artifact card pipeline
High morale = larger hand = more set combinations
Scales well — opponent-independent path to stars

summary.title: Recruit Synergy
bullets: Consider how ethical + hidden recruits interact
Shared faction = doubled allegiance benefits
Best games hinge on explosive recruit combo moments

summary.title: Worker Placement as Denial
bullets: Block key spaces opponents need
Denial can be as valuable as the action itself
Especially effective in 2-3 player games

summary.title: Retrieval Timing
bullets: Retrieve early to surprise opponents and reset position
First access to contested spaces in the next cycle
Trade fewer current actions for positional advantage

summary.title: Morale Investment
bullets: Higher morale = larger hand = more artifact/recruit flexibility
Invest early if planning artifact or recruit strategies
Avoid zero morale unless deliberately built for it

summary.title: Reading the Table
bullets: Track opponents' faction alignments, market contributions, artifact collections
Predict allegiance moves and market completions
Seeing the end 2 turns early = decisive positioning

summary.title: The Blitz Endgame
bullets: Chain multiple star placements in 1-2 cycles (e.g., 6 -> 10)
Stockpile resources and cards, then strike at once
Give opponents no time to react — the best revolutions are sudden

entries.definition: Your 10 victory tokens. Place all 10 to win. Placed by constructing markets, trading artifact sets, allegiance milestones, recruit abilities, and special actions.
A die in your player color representing one of your workers. Face value = knowledge level. You start with 2 and can recruit up to 4 total.
Determines your knowledge threshold — the maximum combined dice total your workers can have before the smartest one escapes during retrieval.
Determines your hand size for recruit and artifact cards. Higher morale = more cards in hand. Dropping to zero causes penalties and forced discards.
After retrieving workers, sum all worker dice values. If total exceeds your knowledge threshold, your highest-value worker escapes to the unused pool.
One of two turn options. Roll an available worker die and place it on an action space to perform that space's action.
One of two turn options. Take ALL workers off the board, roll them all, then perform the knowledge check.
When you place a worker matching another worker's die value on the same space, you gain a bonus action or resource. Works with your own or opponents' dice.
One of four factions. Territory produces Energy (basic resource) and Gold (commodity). Represents the gleaming city elite.
One of four factions. Territory produces Food (basic resource) and Stone (commodity). Represents the underground dwellers.
One of four factions. Territory produces Water (basic resource) and Clay (commodity). Represents the desert survivors.
One of four factions. Cloud territory produces Bliss (basic resource) and provides access to artifact cards. Represents the sky-dwelling dreamers.
Each faction has an allegiance track. As it advances through tunnel progress and other actions, milestones trigger bonuses for all players with aligned recruits.
Each faction has a tunnel being dug. Players place workers to advance progress. When completed, contributors gain bonuses and the faction's allegiance milestone triggers.
Basic resource produced in the Euphorian territory. Used to acquire Gold (commodity) and contribute to certain market constructions.
Basic resource produced in the Subterran territory. Used to acquire Stone (commodity) and contribute to certain market constructions.
Basic resource produced in the Wastelander territory. Used to acquire Clay (commodity) and contribute to certain market constructions.
Basic resource produced in the Icarite cloud territory. Used to trade for artifact cards and perform special Icarite actions. Keeping citizens blissful and ignorant.
Commodity produced in the Euphorian territory by trading Energy. Used for market construction and some star-placement actions.
Commodity produced in the Subterran territory by trading Food. Used for market construction and some star-placement actions.
Commodity produced in the Wastelander territory by trading Water. Used for market construction and some star-placement actions.
Cards collected from the Icarite territory and other sources. Come in 6 types. Trade specific sets at the Artifact Market to place an authority star.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
One of six artifact card types. Part of sets traded at the Artifact Market for authority star placement.
Board location where you trade complete sets of artifact cards to place an authority star. Different locations may require different set combinations.
Constructed cooperatively by players during the game. Contributors place an authority star. Non-contributors suffer a permanent negative penalty.
A permanent negative effect suffered by players who did not contribute to a completed market. Examples: losing access to spaces, paying double for commodities.
A board space with a face-down market tile. Players place workers and pay resources to contribute. When enough resources are paid, the market is completed and the tile is revealed.
Your face-up recruit card with a publicly known, always-active ability. Aligned with one of the four factions.
Your face-down recruit card with a secret ability. Can be revealed when its trigger condition is met, granting a powerful one-time or ongoing effect.
The specific condition that must be met before you can reveal your hidden recruit. Conditions vary by card and might involve resources, allegiance, or board position.
Action spaces in Euphorian, Subterran, and Wastelander territories where workers are placed to gain basic resources (Energy, Food, Water).
Action spaces where basic resources are traded for commodities (Energy->Gold, Food->Stone, Water->Clay).
Action spaces in the Icarite cloud territory where workers gain Bliss and access artifact-related actions.
Tokens placed on faction tunnel tracks to mark progress. As miners advance, tunnel milestones are reached and allegiance bonuses trigger.
When the knowledge check fails (dice sum exceeds threshold), the highest-value worker leaves — returned to the unused pool. A major setback that reduces your action economy.
Special cards that present moral choices during the game, often with tradeoffs between immediate benefit and long-term consequences.
Tokens used on certain board spaces to track resource generation multipliers or other scaling effects.
Where your 3rd and 4th worker dice wait before being recruited, and where escaped workers return. Workers can be re-recruited from here through specific game actions.
Each of the four board regions (Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander, Icarite) with its own resource production, commodity, tunnel track, and construction sites.
